edges.description: Without the activator, glucosylceramide cannot be degraded and accumulates.
edges.source: Saposin C Deficiency from PSAP Variants

edges.description: Glucosylceramide storage in macrophages produces Gaucher-like reticuloendothelial infiltration and hepatosplenomegaly.
intermediate_mechanisms: lipid-laden storage macrophages in the reticuloendothelial system

edges.description: Reticuloendothelial and marrow storage disease can reduce circulating platelets.

edges.target: Saposin C Deficiency from PSAP Variants

nodes.description: Visceral organomegaly from glucosylceramide-laden storage macrophages.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype Expansion for Atypical Gaucher Disease Due to Homozygous Missense PSAP Variant in a Large Consanguineous Pakistani Family.

nodes.description: Undegraded glucosylceramide accumulates in macrophage lysosomes, producing lipid-laden storage macrophages (Gaucher-like cells) in the reticuloendothelial system.

evidence.reference_title: Deficiency of Glucocerebrosidase Activity beyond Gaucher Disease: PSAP and LIMP-2 Dysfunctions.
evidence.snippet: impairment of GCase activity can be rarely caused by SapC or LIMP-2 deficiencies

meta.association: Biallelic PSAP variants abolishing saposin C

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype Expansion for Atypical Gaucher Disease Due to Homozygous Missense PSAP Variant in a Large Consanguineous Pakistani Family.
evidence.snippet: Atypical Gaucher disease is caused by variants in the PSAP gene.

nodes.description: Saposin C arises from proteolytic cleavage of prosaposin (encoded by PSAP). Biallelic PSAP variants abolish saposin C, the activator protein that glucocerebrosidase requires to access and degrade glucosylceramide. Enzyme and GBA1 gene are normal; the defect is a missing activator cofactor.
nodes.id: Saposin C Deficiency from PSAP Variants

evidence.reference_title: Deficiency of Glucocerebrosidase Activity beyond Gaucher Disease: PSAP and LIMP-2 Dysfunctions.
evidence.snippet: Glucocerebrosidase (GCase) is a lysosomal enzyme that catalyzes the breakdown of glucosylceramide in the presence of its activator saposin C (SapC).

evidence.reference_title: Non-neuronopathic Gaucher disease due to saposin C deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Saposin (SAP-) C is required for glucosylceramide degradation, and its deficiency results in a variant form of Gaucher disease.

nodes.description: Thrombocytopenia from hypersplenism and marrow involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype Expansion for Atypical Gaucher Disease Due to Homozygous Missense PSAP Variant in a Large Consanguineous Pakistani Family.
